comment.body:
JP was more dovish than what the market expected, so those positions were unwound as I warned you, but tbh i didn't expect this much of a squeeze out of the IC... but this was my bad, bcs the model showed that possibility, I just misjudged its probability...
however, anyone who placed stop-losses to the weekly range, was deep in profit after the predicted tuesday/wednesday 130+ pts selloff, and could switch to calls when JP sent the green signal, take profit and let the others run...
System is everything

comment.body:
and shifting towards the 2nd half of Sept as I wrote you many times in the past two weeks.
you know why... inflation is up, tariffs related cost push inflation is there, but JP is leaning towards cut due to labor market weakness + political pressure... 
2+2 = not good news, hawkish cut

plus, this momentum has a fuel due to the narrative of coming cut, and when the event passed, what will they say? ok. DJ is creative, but it won't be a QE cycle...
this is crucial

comment.body:
and when one does, like swing positions, 
stop-losses and profit taking is crucial.
like this week: 
downside target was hit, i took a huge profit, and let the remaining positions running + intraday puts
placed my stop-loss slightly above the weekly lower bound (vol adjusted distance... wrote about it in the educational post), and it still got out green when the pop happened.
I didn't trade the upside move. 
the goal is to make money, not to trade.

comment.body:
1) no preparation, no planning
- you don't read carefully the post, don't mark the ranges and don't build a plan what you will do with these infos: "if this, then that"
trading is about adaptation, in shitty risky markets like this one.
bcs this is not investor-market, this is traders/manipulators-market.
You take 2 mins to scroll through the post, miss key infos, bcs all you want is to somebody trade for you.
If you don't build a consistent plan, and don't learn to be sticked to it, you will lose. 
No royal road. 

(and even when I posted trades with like 91% accuracy, some ppl still lost... so it is  not me)

user.name: Alma
user.bio: Privat investor & speculator | Mathematics | Self-made quant | Market researcher | Options and Futures trader

comment.body:
2) overtrading
- first of all, somebody said earlier in the chat that intraday trading is not for everybody.
I could not agree more... 
Intraday trading is the hardest thing to do, and not bcs of knowledge, but bcs of the uneducated/untrained mind. 
And there are ppl who are simply not tailored to do it. And it is okay. Charlie Munger, Ray Dalio etc. also weren't good day traders. They still made money, and had a nice life. 
You have to find yourself, whats your style, where are you the most effective, etc. 
There are different styles...

I see ppl here trading only bcs they want to trade, not bcs of making some money. I keep warning you again and again, if you watch carefully, i write it in posts, I write it in the first messages of the daily chat-threads etc.
... in vain (for the ppl who should read it)
Not too much difference from gambling-addiction.

Take a stepback, do some self-reflection, and ask yourself, whats your goal, why are you doing this... ;))

user.name: Alma
user.bio: Privat investor & speculator | Mathematics | Self-made quant | Market researcher | Options and Futures trader

comment.body:
3) using assets you don't know
- one of my main principle is to trade with assets only if i know.
I see guys trading options only bcs they see this is cool and popular among "big dawgs" on the soc media and it promises big and fast alpha, passing the options test of the brokerage with chatGPT and boom, jump into it without any clue...
It does, if you use it carefully.
I will write educational posts about that, but there are a lot of free materials about it. 
It is absolutely your business who you spend your money, but the thing is, anything can work.
And Wall Street, soc media etc. is a big lie. They want your money. they will give you unuseful courses etc for hundreds of dollars, you will pay them and still lose on the market, bcs you want others to do the efforts for you. 
if you buy the books i recommended (or just download, bcs they are available on the web for free btw) you will know everything these courses shill to you for an overpriced price.

you are not forced to spend your money irresponsibly, and lose it on an asset you don't even know how it works... (i.e. option convexity...)
But i warn you to be honest with yourself and don't do it
We are all adults

comment.body:
+1) responsibility
I see 1-2 people here coming out only when the market went towards the lower probability scenario, and start to post sarcastic things like "oh, so this is the low prob scenario??? loool", "this bitch was wrong again loool" etc.
First of all, nobody needs this demoralisation, so next time they will get kicked from chatting. Ok? Ok.
Seconds... read the 1st point I just wrote + you are not forced to be here for 35 dollar per month.
Market is a game of probabilities. You either can build your own trading system, or not. But take responsibility for your decisions or for the lack of decisions you made.
...and come out when i predict something and it comes true too ;))

user.name: Alma
user.bio: Privat investor & speculator | Mathematics | Self-made quant | Market researcher | Options and Futures trader

comment.body:
To beat the market/or just get some profit you need to be planned, patient and focused.
This is something nobody can teach you. It is something everybody must deal with by themselves.
Soc media is full of shit about trading.
I try to write about it, warn you day by day, but if the ppl who should read it, doesn't do, and doesn't take time to just think about it, it is all in vain.
And this substack is not a "kindergarten", this is a community of traders, investors... 
I'm not here to repeat the same warnings day after day... we are here to share thoughts, and I'm here to share what the models tell, that are still more accurate than most of the furus out-there.
As one of the market maker member said here: "i never seen anybody reading the market like Alma does"
ok, end of self-advertisement.

Also I'm not sure that it is clear to everybody here, what he is paying for... that there is a website/a blog/substack page, where you find educational posts, older posts etc.
Make sure you know what you are paying for. 
I help with constantly searching posts for you, re-link them in the posts, but not many ppl click on them.

So pls, be a bit more adults... we all want adult discussions here

comment.body:
For what it’s worth, something similar happened to me 
I expected a positive reaction as my default because the market was too hedged short term (simple VIX 9d > VIX as one example of course plus what we could see in the chain). So my default was if he was even “standard” Powell vs stern cop Powell the reaction would be positive 
Like you i thought the ccs would hold or at least be a battle zone on the way to 6450 at most 
In hindsight looking back the larger position at 6475 near the all time high was such a low delta at say 9:00 that I miss read the upward convexity
